Copied

The PeopleГўВҖВҷs Republic of China is tied with the U.S. on the gold medal leaderboard as of Sunday, August 4. Team China won a number of competitions on day 9 of the Paris Olympics, including the menГўВҖВҷs 100-meter freestyle medley relay, which saw the end of the U.S.ГўВҖВҷ 40-year-long reign over the race.

The thrilling heat saw a photo finish, with the Chinese teamГўВҖВҷs anchor and 100-meter freestyle gold medalist, Pan Zhanle, overtaking the U.S. swimmer in the final leg of the race to bring home the gold for China.

ChinaГўВҖВҷs womenГўВҖВҷs team also won bronze in the 100-meter freestyle medley relay in a competition that saw the U.S. break the world record to win gold.

Not far behind the U.S. and China was the French team, who took home bronze and nearly won the competition themselves.

Team China also continued their dominance in table tennis after Fan Zhendong won gold in the menГўВҖВҷs singles competition, joining fellow compatriot, Chen Meng, from the womenГўВҖВҷs team to win the Olympic competition.

Zhengdong cemented his legacy as one of the greatest table tennis players in Olympic history after winning the singles competition, joining fellow Chinese greats like Ma Long, Zhang Like and Wang Hao to win multiple medals.

In menГўВҖВҷs artistic gymnastics, ChinaГўВҖВҷs Liu Yang won the gold in the rings competition, with fellow countryman You Jingyuan winning silver.

The Chinese menГўВҖВҷs and womenГўВҖВҷs gymnastics team has now won six medals, including one gold and four silvers.

ChinaГўВҖВҷs womenГўВҖВҷs boxing team is hoping to secure their own golds in the coming days, following 75kg boxer Li Qian advanced to the semifinal, guaranteeing herself an Olympic medal.

In addition to Qian, ChinaГўВҖВҷs Chang Yuan will compete for the 63.5kg gold medal in womenГўВҖВҷs boxing after advancing to the finals on Sunday, August 4.

WomenГўВҖВҷs olympic boxing will also have its first ever medalist from the Refugee Olympic Team after boxer Djankeu Ngamba reached the 75kg weightГўВҖВҷs semifinals - she is guaranteed at least a bronze medal.

In menГўВҖВҷs tennis, the young Spaniard, Carlos Alcaraz, faced off against the legendary Novak Djokovic of Serbia, in what was a rematch of the 2024 Wimbledon championship.

However, this time around, it was Djokovic who won the tournament, bringing home gold for Serbia.

Finally, in the highly anticipated menГўВҖВҷs 100 meter dash, the U.S.ГўВҖВҷ Noah Lyles edged out JamaicaГўВҖВҷs Kishane Thompson to win gold by less than one-one hundredth of a second.

For more, check out our exclusive content onCGTN Nowand subscribe to our weekly newsletter,The China Report.
